title: Sources: Ravens pivot, agree to add Hendrickson
content: The Ravens have agreed to sign Trey Hendrickson, sources told ESPN, in a high-priced pivot after their canceled blockbuster trade for Maxx Crosby.

title: Live tracking NFL free agency: Updates on signings, trades, cuts
content: Players can officially sign at 4 p.m. ET Wednesday. We're following all NFL free agency moves as they happen.

title: Syracuse fires Autry after 3 tourney-less years
content: Syracuse has fired men's basketball coach Adrian Autry after the Orange did not make the NCAA tournament in his three seasons at the helm.

title: U.S. in jeopardy at WBC after upset loss to Italy
content: Italy stunned Team USA with an 8-6 victory in World Baseball Classic pool play Tuesday night, one of the biggest upsets in the tournament's 20-year history that puts the Americans in jeopardy of not advancing to the knockout round.

title: Sources: Ravens nixed Crosby deal after physical
content: The Ravens have "backed out" of their agreement to acquire five-time Pro Bowl defensive end Maxx Crosby, with sources telling ESPN that medical concerns arose after a physical.

title: Source: Commanders agree with LB Chaisson
content: The Washington Commanders have agreed to sign K'Lavon Chaisson to a one-year deal worth $12 million, according to NFL Network, adding another edge rusher to their overhauled pass rush.

title: Source: NFL exploring Thanksgiving Eve game
content: The NFL is exploring the possibility of playing a game the day before Thanksgiving, a source told ESPN.

title: Celtics' Brown ejected, then takes to social media
content: Celtics guard Jaylen Brown was ejected in the second quarter of Tuesday's matchup at the Spurs after officials whistled him for back-to-back technical fouls.

title: 'Wilt, me, then Kobe': Adebayo scores 83 points
content: Bam Adebayo scored the most points since Wilt Chamberlain's iconic 100-point game in 1962 as the Heat star went for 83 points in a blowout win over the Wizards.

title: WNBA, union log marathon talks without deal
content: The WNBA and its players' union did not reach a deal on a new collective bargaining agreement after a marathon, 12-hour bargaining session.

title: Sources: Pats land Packers' Doubs on 4-year deal
content: The Patriots have agreed to sign former Packers wide receiver Romeo Doubs to a four-year deal worth up to $80 million, sources told ESPN.

title: Sources: TE Knox stays with Bills on new deal
content: The Bills found a way to keep tight end Dawson Knox on the 2026 roster amid a difficult cap situation by signing him to a new three-year contract, sources told ESPN's Jeremy Fowler on Tuesday.

title: Texans bolster O-line, reach deal with OT Smith
content: Free agent offensive tackle Braden Smith reached agreement with the Houston Texans on a two-year, $25 million contract that includes $13.5 million fully guaranteed, his agent told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Source: WR Raymond leaves Lions to join Bears
content: Kalif Raymond has agreed to sign a one-year contract with the Bears, reuniting the wide receiver with Ben Johnson, a source told ESPN's Jeremy Fowler.

title: Source: LB Lloyd gets $45M deal with Panthers
content: Former Jaguars linebacker Devin Lloyd agreed to a three-year, $45 million deal with the Panthers, a source told ESPN's Adam Schefter on Monday.

title: Sources: Tua to sign 1-year deal with Falcons
content: Former Miami Dolphins quarterback Tua Tagovailoa plans to sign a one-year deal with the Atlanta Falcons, a source told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Commanders land Oweh on 4-year, $100M deal
content: Former Chargers defensive end Odafe Oweh, who has 17.5 sacks since 2024, has agreed with the Commanders on a four-year, $100 million deal, his agents told ESPN.

title: Sources: QB Willis, Fins reach 3-year, $67.5M deal
content: Free agent QB Malik Willis, after reviving his career with the Packers, has agreed to a three-year, $67.5 million deal with the Dolphins, sources told ESPN.

title: NWSL ambition rankings: Which teams are raising standards, and which aren't trying enough?
content: The NWSL has teams that are building stadiums, signing stars and spending money. Meanwhile, other teams, well, aren't doing any of that.

content: A lot of red cards were handed out in Cruzeiro's win over Atlético-MG, but it's nowhere near the world record.

title: Woman arrested on the charge of honey trapping businessmen
content: The police are on the look-out for her accomplice who is said to have conspired with her to honey trap rich people

content: Consumers in several districts, including Patna, Kaimur, Darbhanga, Motihari and Gaya, say they have been facing delays of up to five days in getting cylinders

title: Brewing coffee for work, crafting poetry for life
content: A first-year BA English student, Sabari has authored his debut poetry collection in his very first semester in college

title: Students should not depend excessively on AI for research: KSOU V-C
content: The UGC has introduced Research Ethics as a subject in research programmes to curb issues relating to plagiarism

title: Groundbreaking ceremony for ArcelorMittal/Nippon Steel plant on March 23, says Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu
content: Company plans to establish a state-of-the-art 17.80 MTPA Integrated Steel Plant in two phases at Rajayyapeta in Anakapalli district, and the first phase will have a production capacity of 8.20 MTPA; it invites Expression of Interest for establishment of a dedicated 1-million-tonne steel fabrication unit

title: Vontimitta gears up for grand Sita Rama Kalyanam on April 1
content: Ancient Kodandarama temple in Kadapa district prepares for the state-sponsored Srirama Navami celebrations beginning March 26

title: Kharge seeks PM’s reply in Parliament, alleges ‘fake source-based assurances’ on West Asia crisis

title: A.P. CM Naidu proposes Indian School of Agriculture to render AI-based tech services to farmers
content: A 90-day drive for water conservation, including desilting of water bodies, to begin on April 1;  special programme to be held on the 9th day of every month in all districts to resolve land disputes

title: Red Fort blast case: Delhi Court extends police custody of two accused
content: Special Judge Pitambar Dutt extended the NIA custody of Zameer Ahmad Ahangar and Tufail Ahmad Bhat after conducting the proceedings in camera

title: Resolution seeking removal of Om Birla as Speaker defeated by voice vote in Lok Sabha
content: Responding to the two-day-long debate, Mr. Shah asserted that the House will be run by its own rules and not by the rules of a party

title: Boxing divisional rankings: Jai Opetaia keeps top spot and claims new title
content: Jai Opetaia beat Brandon Glanton to keep his No. 1 spot at cruiserweight. Here are the latest ESPN divisional rankings.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Bernardo Silva could join Messi at Inter Miami
content: Manchester City's Bernardo Silva could be joining Lionel Messi at Inter Miami. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Lakhs gather for Suguturu Gangamma jatara in Punganur
content: Descendants of the erstwhile Punganur zamindars join festivities as palace temple opens for annual public darshan

title: CAG reports expose NDA government’s ‘financial indiscipline’, says Jagan
content: The government owes an explanation for the declining revenues and mounting fiscal deficit, he says, while dismissing as baseless and motivated the allegations on Tirupati laddu issue

title: DC presents Best Service Awards to government employees
content: The DC says recognising government employees with awards would motivate them to work with greater enthusiasm and commitment

title: Here are the big stories from Karnataka today
content: Welcome to the Karnataka Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today. Curated and written by Nalme Nachiyar.

title: Animal Husbandry department launches digital platform for livestock and pet trading
content: The objective is to benefit both farmers and traders by overcoming the limitations of traditional market systems

title: Governor Ravi bids farewell to Tamil Nadu; State Cabinet stays away
content: Kerala Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar, who has been appointed to additionally discharge functions of Tamil Nadu Governor, is set to assume office at the Lok Bhavan in Chennai on March 12

title: Karnataka police tackle Kerala lottery sales in Mysuru-Chamarajanagar border areas
content: The district police say they registered two cases on March 9 against persons illegally selling Kerala State lottery tickets in Yelandur town

title: NH-66 works near completion in Kannur and Kasaragod; target set before monsoon
content: Key works are progressing on the Cherkala-Nileshwaram and Nileshwaram-Taliparamba reaches of the highway

title: Stalin requests Union government to revisit methodology to supply adequate gas to meet power demand
content: CM has written to Union Minister for Petroleum and Natural Gas Hardeep Singh Puri regarding the natural gas allocation to power plants

title: LPG shortage crisis: Karnataka Assembly witnesses uproar as Congress and BJP trade barbs
content: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah blamed Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s weak foreign policy, triggering protests from BJP members

title: Archaeological exploration along Pambai river in Villupuram yields Iron Age cultural materials
content: Surface-level field investigations have yielded significant finds such as Iron Age urn burials, potsherds, glass beads, ring wells and ceramics, indicating the potential for full-scale excavations in the region

title: Rural tap water connections rose from 17% to 82% since launch of Jal Jeevan Mission: President
content: Addressing the Jal Mahotsav programme, the President said water is not merely a basic facility in India but is deeply connected to the country’s culture, traditions, livelihoods and community life

title: Used phone buyers urged to check sale of blacklisted products
content: Kozhikode city police trace 124 lost or stolen phones blacklisted on Sanchar Saathi portal and distribute it to the original owners

title: Over 93,000 posts vacant in CAPFs: govt. tells Rajya Sabha
content: The Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) has the highest number of 28,342 vacant posts followed by the Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) with 27,400 vacancies; MoS Home says govt. has reduced the time taken for medical examinations and lowered cut-off marks for shortlisting candidates in categories facing shortfalls to ensure adequate recruitment

title: UP Police recover over 500 stolen LPG cylinders in Jhansi, arrest 7
content: The police also recovered ₹11.10 lakh in cash and ₹83,820 believed to be proceeds from the sale of cylinders, from their possession, he said

title: Uttam Nagar Holi clash: Delhi HC stays MCD demolition for a week
content: Court allows residents to file fresh petitions as civic body says action targeted encroachments, not specific individuals

title: Census 2027 to also include transgender-headed households, Rajya Sabha told
content: Nityanand Rai said enumerators shall undertake house-to-house field visits in the entire enumeration area for the collection of Census data to avoid any exclusion on any ground

title: RERA tribunal upholds description of BDA as a ‘promoter’, crucial win for allottees of sites in Bengaluru
content: BDA had submitted that site allotments are contractual in nature and governed by the BDA (Allotment of Site) Rules, 1984, and that disputes should be adjudicated under those rules

title: Coimbatore’s e-learning initiative on marine life
content: An e-learning opportunity that connects classrooms with the wonders and challenges of marine life and its conservation

title: PM Modi highlights Kerala’s potential in floating solar power sector to enhance clean energy and infrastructure development
content: Modi says the 50-MW floating solar project for which foundation stone has been laid at West Kallada is a step towards this direction

title: Periodic revision of guidance value essential for fair land compensation: Karnataka Minister Krishna Byre Gowda
content: In some areas, the guidance value of land may be around ₹2 lakh, while the actual market value ranges between ₹70 lakh and ₹80 lakh, said the Minister

title: Artist Jitish Kallat to chair curator selection for next edition of Kochi-Muziris Biennale
content: Jitish Kallat, who lives and works in Mumbai, is a member of the Board of Trustees of the Kochi Biennale Foundation. He previously served as curator of the second edition of the Kochi–Muziris Biennale (2014–15), titled Whorled Explorations

title: LPG cylinder shortage: Nearly 90% of hotels in Shivamogga to close down operations on March 12
content: The Shivamogga City Hotel Owners’ Association, which met in Shivamogga on March 11, discussed the shortage of LPG cylinders at length

title: Alleging Kerala’s neglect in PM Modi’s event, LDF protests and symbolically opens Talapady-Chengala NH stretch in Kasaragod
content: Leaders said they were dedicating the road to the public “without waiting for a formal inauguration”, asserting that the project was completed through the efforts and intervention of the State government

title: All you need to know about: passive euthanasia
content: The principles of passive euthanasia have been practically applied by the apex court for the first time

title: No-confidence motion debate: Heated exchange between Rahul Gandhi and Ravi Shankar Prasad in Parliament, Rahul reiterates ‘PM compromised’ jibe
content: Refuting Mr. Gandhi’s claims, Mr. Prasad asserted, “I like to remind the LoP that PM Narendra Modi can never be compromised. I will add one more thing: Is the debate allowed in point of order? It is not.”

title: Air India Express Hyderabad–Phuket flight suffers nose-wheel damage after hard landing, Phuket airport runway shut
content: The flight made a hard landing on arrival, damaging its nose wheel and leaving the aircraft stranded on the airport’s only runway

title: PM Modi holds roadshow in Kochi ahead of ₹10,800-crore project inauguration
content: Modi waved at party workers and supporters as he travelled in an open vehicle from near the entrance of the stadium to the venue of the inauguration of the developmental projects

title: Wife among four arrested for murder of priest in Srirangapatna
content: The police learnt that his wife was allegedly in an illicit relationship with Harish, a tipper lorry driver from the same village

title: Women riders from the Northeast champion a cleaner Himalayas
content: Backed by Royal Enfield Social Mission, seven riders from Northeast India promote sustainable waste practices in mountain communities

title: Ex-diplomat Taranjit Singh Sandhu sworn in as Delhi L-G
content: Delhi High Court Chief Justice Devendra Kumar Upadhyaya administered the oath of office and secrecy to Mr. Sandhu

title: SC passive euthanasia verdict: What happened to Harish Rana?
content: This is the first time the Supreme Court has allowed the use of passive euthanasia to alleviate the suffering of a person

title: LPG crisis: Kerala CM urges Centre to subsidise cooking gas
content: Pinarayi Vijayan says Centre’s decision to hike cooking gas prices, including for households, will exacerbate the cost-of-living crisis and upend family budgets

title: Meghalaya CM announces postponement of April 10 polls to Garo Hills council due to violence
content: The situation in West Garo Hills remained calm but tense on Wednesday (March 11, 2026), with the district administration extending the curfew till midnight of March 13 and continuing the suspension of mobile internet services, officials said

title: Ground-breaking for AM/NS steel plant on March 23: Andhra CM Chandrababu Naidu
content: “The Kadapa Steel Plant works would begin soon,” CM Chandrababu Naidu said, and expressed the government’s wish to complete them both by March 2028

title: CPI(M) terms Minister Riyas’ exclusion from PM Modi’s Kochi event ‘an instance of BJP’s political vendetta’ against Kerala
content: CPI(M) State secretary M.V. Govindan says Centre’s “right-wing politics-driven discrimination” against Kerala was “highly evident” in the NH-66 episode

title: Former panchayat president hacked to death by gang in Virudhunagar district
content: When he was near Villipathiri, an armed gang waylaid him at around 9 p.m. They pushed him off the bike and attacked him with weapons. He died on the spot, and the gang fled the scene

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election: Sowmiya Anbumani likely to enter the fray in a PMK stronghold
content: Highly placed sources in the PMK reiterated that Dr. Anbumani has a “very good equation” with the AIADMK and BJP and has managed to rule out the inclusion of his father S. Ramadoss in the National Democratic Alliance

title: Multi-thousand crore GainBitcoin scam case: CBI arrests Darwin Labs co-founder Ayush Varshney
content: The CBI is conducting the investigation pursuant to the Supreme Court order dated December 13, 2023, involving the FIRs registered in various parts of the country

title: Andhra CM Chandrababu Naidu to review flagship schemes at Collectors’ Conference
content: Chandrababu Naidu will review implementation of various flagship schemes of the coalition government and maintenance of law and order among other things

title: LPG shortage in country; 1 crore people to be unemployed: Kejriwal
content: Kejriwal accused Prime Minister Modi of "demolishing" India's non-aligned foreign policy of several decades and making Iran our "enemy"

title: Minister’s ‘exclusion’ from PM’s event: LDF holds parallel inauguration of NH-66 Ramanattukara-Vengalam stretch in Kerala’s Kozhikode
content: Roadshow led by Minister Mohammed Riyas taken out in protest against his alleged exclusion from official inaugural event attended by Prime Minister Narendra Modi in Kochi

title: NCERT book row: SC objects to ‘rewritten’ chapter a day after NCERT apologises on section about ‘corruption in judiciary’
content: The court directed that the chapter, if revised, would not be published unless it was approved by a committee of domain experts; it ordered the government to constitute the expert committee

title: Air India, its subsidiary to operate 58 flights to and from West Asia
content: While Air India will operate eight flights to and from Jeddah, Air India Express will be operating 14 scheduled flights to and from Muscat

title: Heatwave conditions likely in parts of Maharashtra, rain predicted in three division
content: According to IMD officials, a cyclonic circulation over South Jharkhand and adjoining North Chhattisgarh, combined with a trough line extending through Vidarbha, is influencing the weather pattern

title: 108 Maoists surrender in Chhattisgarh
content: Among the surrendered guerrillas, six were divisional commanders carrying a reward of eight lakh each

title: Additional measures to ensure domestic LPG supplies likely
content: Increase in spacing between two supplies, supply of refills with 10-kg cooking gas may be evaluated if the situation West Asia war triggered prolongs

title: Madras High Court urged to take suo motu cognisance of sexual offences against children in T.N.
content: Chief Justice’s Bench asks a 73-year-old advocate to file a PIL petition based on available materials and says, it will consider it appropriately

title: KSRTC buses arranged for devotees to visit Gopalaswamy Betta in Gundlupet for annual jatra on March 15
content: Devotees arriving in two-wheelers, four-wheelers and other private vehicles, except KSRTC buses, must park their vehicles at the Forest Department check-post

title: Eastern Railway felicitates women workforce for exemplary service
content: Eastern Railway appreciated women holding critical job roles in the railways like loco-pilots, guards, station managers, ticket collectors, booking clerks and more

title: RCB vs SRH to kick off IPL 2026 at Chinnaswamy on March 28 - Full schedule inside
content: IPL 2026 kicks off March 28 with Royal Challengers Bengaluru facing Sunrisers Hyderabad in a Bengaluru opener. The BCCI announced the first 20 days of the schedule, featuring 20 matches across 10 venues. Full schedule awaits state election dates.

title: Opposition's no-trust motion against Speaker Om Birla defeated in Lok Sabha
content: Lok Sabha rejected a no-confidence motion against Speaker Om Birla by voice vote amid opposition protests demanding Home Minister Amit Shah's apology. Shah criticized the move as rare and regrettable, emphasizing the Speaker's neutrality and the House's adherence to its own rules, not party dictates. Protests continued, disrupting proceedings.

title: Amazon orders 90-day reset, what new policy means for engineers and other top leaders
content: Amazon is tightening its code review and deployment rules for engineers following recent major outages, some linked to AI tools. A 90-day safety reset mandates dual reviews for changes to critical systems. This move comes after incidents caused millions in lost orders, prompting a top-down audit of code management practices.

title: 'King of the world' statue of Trump & Epstein in Titanic pose appears near US Capitol
content: A provocative sculpture, "King of the World," featuring Donald Trump and Jeffrey Epstein in a Titanic pose, was installed on the National Mall.  The artwork, by activist group Secret Handshake, criticizes their alleged ties, accompanied by banners urging "Make America Safe Again." This installation highlights renewed scrutiny of Trump's past association with Epstein amid ongoing document releases.

title: Moon samples, 350 kg rover, 100-day mission life: Isro chief outlines Chandrayaan-4, 5 plans
content: India's ISRO is planning ambitious future missions, including Chandrayaan-4 for lunar sample return and Chandrayaan-5 with a heavier, longer-lasting lander. Future endeavors also encompass Mars landing and Venus missions, alongside the Gaganyaan human spaceflight program aiming for astronaut launches within two years.

title: USCIS issues 'alert', shares details of the form it will reject starting April 1
content: USCIS has updated Form I-129, effective April 1, 2026, requiring employers to detail educational and experience requirements for H-1B visa applicants. This change aims to align with the Department of Labor's wage level assignments and verify consistency between the Labor Condition Application and the registration process, impacting the upcoming FY27 H1B cap season.

title: Shah Rukh Khan features again on Hurun Global Rich List along with Taylor Swift, Rihanna, Jay Z
content: Bollywood superstar Shah Rukh Khan has once again made it to the Hurun Global Rich List with a net worth of USD 1.3 billion, solidifying his status as a major business figure. While his wealth saw a slight dip from last year, he remains among the world's wealthiest actors. Film producer Namit Malhotra also debuted on the list.

title: Khushboo Sundar on World Cup trophy being taken to temple: 'The trophy is for India'
content: Actor and politician Khushboo Sundar emphasized that the World Cup trophy belongs to all of India, urging people to view the win as national pride, not a religious matter. She defended taking the trophy to places of worship, citing cricketers' personal expressions of gratitude and devotion. Read on to know more in detail.

title: 1st ODI: Pakistan humbled in Bangladesh; hosts chase target in 15.1 overs
content: Bangladesh crushed Pakistan by eight wickets in the first ODI to take a 1-0 series lead. Pakistan collapsed to 114 after a strong start, with Nahid Rana taking five wickets and Mehidy Hasan Miraz three. In reply, Tanzid Hasan Tamim smashed 67 not out, helping Bangladesh chase the target comfortably in 15.1 overs. Pakistan’s bowlers struggled to create pressure.

title: Day after Anthropic sued the Trump admin, Google makes an announcement for Pentagon website
content: Google is equipping the US Department of War with new AI tools, including Agent Designer on GenAI.mil, allowing personnel to create custom digital assistants for administrative tasks. This move expands Google's role in the Pentagon's AI infrastructure. Meanwhile, AI firm Anthropic is suing the Trump administration after being designated a supply chain risk.

title: Japan announces release of strategic oil reserves as Middle East tensions push prices up
content: Japan is releasing significant portions of its oil reserves to stabilize energy markets amidst escalating Middle East tensions. This strategic move, involving both private and state holdings, aims to counter supply concerns and calm global prices. The decision aligns with calls from the International Energy Agency for coordinated action among major economies to mitigate potential shipping disruptions.

title: Big revelation! 'MS Dhoni never said drop Yuvraj Singh': Former selector
content: Former chief selector Sandeep Patil has refuted claims by Yograj Singh that MS Dhoni was responsible for Yuvraj Singh's ouster from the Indian team. Patil stated on record that Dhoni never requested Yuvraj's exclusion and fully trusted the selection committee's decisions. He acknowledged a father's emotions but emphasised the blame was misplaced.

title: Kejriwal appeals to Delhi HC Chief Justice to transfer excise policy case to 'impartial' bench
content: Arvind Kejriwal and other accused in the excise policy case have requested the Delhi High Court Chief Justice to transfer their criminal revision petition from Justice Swarna Kanta Sharma's bench. They allege bias and express concerns over the bench's prima facie view and interim directions without hearing all parties.

title: Why Shivam Dube had to travel by train after India's T20 World Cup glory
content: After India’s 2026 T20 World Cup win, Shivam Dube couldn’t find flights from Ahmedabad to Mumbai, so he travelled by train with his wife and a friend in 3rd AC. To avoid being recognised, he wore a cap and mask and boarded early morning. A ticket checker almost exposed him, but his wife quickly dismissed the suspicion, and the journey stayed smooth.

title: 'Legitimate targets': Iran warns US tech firms including Google, Amazon, Microsoft, Nvidia
content: Iran has warned US tech companies that their offices and infrastructure could become targets as regional conflict escalates. The IRGC-affiliated Tasnim News Agency published a list of locations, including cloud services from Google and Microsoft, allegedly used for military purposes. Iran also threatened to target economic centers and banks linked to US and Israeli entities

title: ‘A welcome step’: Opposition backs TMC’s move to bring impeachment motion against CEC
content: Opposition parties, led by the Trinamool Congress, are planning an impeachment motion against the Chief Election Commissioner over alleged irregularities in West Bengal's electoral roll revision. Citing concerns over a large number of voters under scrutiny, parties like Congress and Samajwadi Party have voiced support.

title: Gaurav Kapur-Kritika Kamra wedding: Farhan Akhtar, Zaheer Khan, Virender Sehwag attend
content: Actress Kritika Kamra and television host Gaurav Kapur are celebrating their wedding in Mumbai with a private ceremony. Close friends and family, including celebrities like Farhan Akhtar, Zaheer Khan, and Sagarika Ghatke, were seen arriving to bless the couple. The intimate celebrations are a departure from a grand affair, focusing on close connections.

title: Naval mines in the Strait of Hormuz? How these cheap but deadly underwater weapons work
content: Concerns are mounting over the Strait of Hormuz as US officials report Iran may be preparing to deploy naval mines. While initial deployments are limited, Iran possesses a substantial mine arsenal, prompting US strikes on Iranian vessels. The strait's critical role in global oil transport makes it vulnerable to such threats.

title: AI Express flight from Hyd makes a hard landing at Phuket, gets stuck on runway; all safe
content: An Air India Express Boeing 737 MAX experienced a hard landing in Phuket, with its nose wheels detaching upon the second touchdown. The aircraft, carrying 138 people, made a bounce after the initial impact. Fortunately, no injuries were reported, and the Directorate General of Civil Aviation is investigating the incident.

title: After T20 WC debacle, Pakistan crumble like a pack of cards vs B'desh
content: Pakistan were bowled out for 114 in 30.4 overs in the first ODI against Bangladesh after starting well with a 41-run opening stand. A sudden middle-order collapse saw wickets tumble quickly. Sahibzada Farhan (27) and Faheem Ashraf (37) were the only notable contributors. Nahid Rana starred with 5/24, while Mehidy Hasan Miraz took 3/29, leaving Bangladesh a modest chase.

title: Hansika Motwani divorces Sohael Khaturiya, no alimony sought
content: Actress Hansika Motwani and businessman Sohael Khaturiya have officially divorced, with the Bandra family court granting their mutual consent petition. The couple, married in December 2022, cited fundamental differences and irreconcilable disagreements as reasons for their separation. They have been living apart since July 2, 2024, and have made no financial or legal claims against each other.

title: Nifty50 closes at 23,866; BSE Sensex tanks 1,342 points - top reasons for fall
content: Stock market crash today: Continuing geopolitical tensions weighed on Nifty50 and BSE Sensex on Wednesday with the indices tanking over 1%. While Nifty50 went below the 24,000 mark, BSE Sensex crashed over 1,000 points.

title: 'I thought my dream was broken': Sanju Samson's emotional confession after T20 World Cup glory
content: Sanju Samson, India's leading run-scorer in the T20 World Cup, expressed profound gratitude for realizing a long-held dream.  Despite initial doubts after a tough series, Samson delivered crucial performances, earning Player of the Tournament. He emphasized fan prayers as more significant than personal accolades, highlighting the unexpected yet fulfilling nature of his success.

title: 'Dare you to score 30% in English': SC junks AI-drafted PIL, schools Ludhiana cloth trader
content: The Supreme Court dismissed a trader's PIL after he admitted using AI tools to draft it and couldn't explain legal terms. Chief Justice Surya Kant questioned the Ludhiana-based hosiery trader's understanding, even offering an "English exam.

title: Man diagnosed with cancer during office health checkup asked to resign
content: A senior manager diagnosed with thyroid cancer was terminated by his employer shortly after his diagnosis. Despite a strong performance record, he was given vague reasons for his dismissal. His subsequent hunger strike highlighted a critical gap in Indian labor laws regarding medical discrimination, prompting his fight for legislative reform.

title: Thank You, Surya! Now India must build for 2028
content: After leading India to the 2026 T20 World Cup, Suryakumar Yadav has set his sights on an Olympic gold in 2028 and a hat-trick of titles. Yet, despite an outstanding captaincy record, questions remain over his form, fitness and longevity. As India look ahead to the Olympics and the next World Cup, it may be time to start building the future under a new leader.

title: Priyanka Chopra on move to Hollywood: ‘I don’t like living in ...
content: Priyanka Chopra Jonas stepped away from Bollywood to pursue Hollywood opportunities. She felt creatively limited in India and sought wider representation for Indian actors.  Her journey was a risk, but she relied on her talent and work ethic.  She aimed to expand her horizons and explore new possibilities in the global entertainment industry.

title: India buys 30 million barrels of Russian crude after US waiver
content: India has secured approximately 30 million barrels of Russian crude following a US waiver, with refiners like Indian Oil and Reliance Industries swiftly acquiring stranded cargoes. This move comes as Middle East disruptions impact traditional oil flows, pushing India to seek alternative supplies. The US clarified the waiver is temporary, aimed at stabilizing global energy markets.

title: ‘Even if you want to laugh ...’: Gambhir explains his serious look after Dhoni’s post
content: Gautam Gambhir finally addressed his serious dugout expressions, explaining the immense pressure of representing India in major tournaments leaves little room for smiles, even after victory.  Responding to MS Dhoni's playful message, Gambhir highlighted the high stakes and expectations, noting that in India, losing is often seen as unacceptable, despite being a part of sports.

title: Indore: Man 'kills' wife, then dies by suicide; sons find bodies after returning home
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Indore's Pardeshipura where a couple was found dead, suspected to be a murder-suicide. Financial distress is believed to be the motive. The sons returned home to find their father hanging and mother deceased, with signs of a struggle. Police are investigating the circumstances surrounding this tragic event.

title: Indian students in Iran begin arranging exit amid safety concerns; first batch set to depart for Armenia border
content: Indian students in Iran are arranging to leave due to escalating conflict fears.  Evacuation routes via Armenia and Azerbaijan are being offered, with some opting for commercial flights back home.  Concerns are high across multiple universities, prompting student associations and political representatives to coordinate safe passage efforts for departing batches.

title: UP teen approaches police to stop partially paralysed father’s 7th marriage
content: A 17-year-old boy in Azamgarh reported his 55-year-old father to the police after being beaten for opposing his seventh marriage. The father, Amarjeet Maurya, has a history of failed marriages, with previous wives leaving due to his refusal to transfer land. The son intervened to protect their ancestral property from further debt incurred for his father's marital pursuits.

title: NCERT chapter row: SC asks to disassociate professors from work paid by public funds
content: The Supreme Court has directed all publicly funded educational institutions to remove Professor Michel Denino and two associates from curriculum and textbook preparation. This decision follows concerns over "offending" content in an NCERT Class 8 social science textbook that allegedly misrepresented the Indian judiciary. The court also suggested the Centre form an expert committee for curriculum review.

title: Iran launches fresh strike on UAE: Two drones fall near Dubai airport; Indian among 4 injured
content: Two drones crashed near Dubai International Airport, injuring four people, including an Indian national. This incident highlights the ongoing regional conflict's impact on vital infrastructure. Despite the disruption, air traffic at the world's busiest international hub continued to operate. The attacks underscore escalating tensions affecting aviation and shipping routes across the Middle East.

title: Rs 23 lakh penalty for maternity leave? Bombay high court steps in
content: The Bombay High Court ruled that maternity leave is not a service break and cannot be penalized under compulsory service bonds for medical professionals. Upholding a dental surgeon's plea, the court declared that contractual obligations cannot override a woman's fundamental right to motherhood and statutory maternity benefits, ensuring career and family balance.

title: US-Iran war: As Hormuz chokes, these 2 pipelines may decide the winner
content: As the Strait of Hormuz faces disruption, Saudi Arabia's East-West and UAE's Habshan-Fujairah pipelines are crucial overland routes for crude oil. These pipelines, designed for emergencies, are now vital shock absorbers for the global economy, preventing a full-blown market panic despite not fully replacing Hormuz's capacity. Their continued operation is paramount.

title: "Mobiles Not Luxury": Raghav Chadha Questions 28-Day "Monthly" Recharges
content: Millions of prepaid mobile users risk being cut off in emergencies due to restrictive recharge rules, Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha said in Parliament on Wednesday.

title: Van Smashes Into Security Barricade Near White House
content: A van slammed into a security barricade near the White House early Wednesday, police said, triggering a full lockdown of downtown Washington.

title: Mumbai's Iconic Hotels Forced to Dim Their Lights As LPG Dry Spell Continues
content: The "City That Never Sleeps" is waking up to a silent crisis not of demand, but of fuel. Mumbai's iconic hospitality sector is currently gasping for air as a severe shortage of commercial LPG cylinders has brought the industry to its knees.

title: 2 Indians Dead, 1 Missing In Middle East War, Confirms Foreign Ministry
content: The spokesperson of the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA), Randhir Jasiwal, on Wednesday confirmed that the two Indian nationals have lost their lives and one still remains missing amid the ongoing Middle Eastern conflict.

title: Parliament Budget Session LIVE Updates: Amit Shah Replies To Debate On No-Confidence Motion Against Speaker

title: Opinion: Opinion | Nitish Kumar, And The Great Bihar Promise That Never Came
content: Nitish Kumar had the chance to transform Bihar. He had built enough political equity and developed administrative chops to bring about real change. Yet, he did not.

title: Missiles Marked "In Memory Of Epstein Island Victims" Fired By Iran Amid War
content: Eleven days into the US and Israel's war on Iran, Tehran is firing retaliatory missiles bearing the words "in memory of victims of Epstein Island" at its targets.

title: 100 Indian Students To Return From Iran Via Armenia Route
content: Amid escalating Israel-Iran tensions, around 100 Indian students stranded in Iran are expected to return to India on March 14 and 15 via commercial flights after travelling to Armenia by road.

title: "Questioning Speaker's Integrity Means Questioning Our Democracy": Amit Shah
content: Union home minister Amit Shah, speaking during the debate on the Opposition's no-confidence motion against Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla, said today that "questioning the Speaker's integrity" is the same as questioning the country's democracy.

title: LPG Black Market Exposed In Bhopal As Iran War Triggers Supply Shortage
content: As thousands of residents in Bhopal stand in long queues for LPG cylinders under the scorching summer heat, NDTV's on-ground investigation has uncovered a disturbing parallel market thriving quietly inside residential neighbourhoods.

title: When Supreme Court First Recognised Concept Of Passive Euthanasia
content: The top court allowed passive euthasia for Harish Rana and directed the All India Institute of Medical Sciences, or AIIMS, to prepare a tailored plan for withdrawal of treatment while ensuring dignity in the process.

title: How Middle East War Has Become A Combat Lab For India's Military Planners
content: The sinking of an Iranian naval vessel on March 4 underscored how the Indian Ocean region is no longer insulated from the spillover effects of major power confrontations.

title: Meta Acquires AI Agent Network Moltbook That Sam Altman Once Mocked
content: Mark Zuckerberg's tech giant Meta Platforms has acquired Moltbook. It is designed as a social network where AI agents can interact with each other, exchange information, and coordinate tasks.

title: JD Vance Mostly Silent On Iran War, Trump Says He's "Philosophically Different"
content: Days before US and Israeli strikes, US Vice President JD Vance said that there was “no chance” of a drawn-out war if the strikes proceeded.

title: How does a trade get canceled? Answering the biggest questions
content: sources tell ESPN that the Baltimore Ravens backed out of a trade that would have sent five-time Pro Bowl pass rusher Maxx Crosby to Baltimore in exchange for two first-round picks . the unraveling of this trade, which could have been made official Wednesday at 4 p.m. ET, will cause ripple effects throughout the league .
